analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet condemns certain individuals as secessionists/traitors, asserts they will be rejected by history and the Chinese people, and states the Chinese Embassy has lodged representations to UK authorities demanding swift investigation and justice. Evaluation by narrative: CH1 (The West is immoral, hostile and decadent) – Partially relevant because the tweet pressures the UK to act, implying wrongdoing occurred on UK soil, but it does not depict the West/UK as immoral or hostile; it focuses on perpetrators, not systemic Western hostility. Therefore, not supported. CH2 (China is a benevolent power) – The tweet shows China using diplomatic/legal channels and appealing for justice, but it is accusatory and punitive rather than emphasizing cooperation, mutual benefit, or multilateralism; weak fit. Not supported. CH3 (China has an epic history) – No historical resilience or past adversities; not supported. CH4 (China has an appropriate political system and honorable values) – Implicit moral stance (branding acts as treason/secession rejected by the people) but no explicit advocacy of China’s political system or values domestically; not supported. CH5 (CCP government succeeds) – No claims about governance performance or development; not supported. CH6 (Culture/nature/heritage) – Not applicable. Conclusion: The tweet does not clearly promote any listed narrative.
